title: Stage 04c — Red Team
description: Adversarial review of what was built. Walks attack surfaces (input boundaries, state, sequence, integrations, auth-edges, resource exhaustion, failure modes, abuse cases, downstream effects, observability gaps), produces concrete reproducers, and triages findings by severity × likelihood × scope. The `must_address_before_peer_review` array names items the implementer MUST address before Stage 5; non-empty → status FAIL. Runs on full + hotfix tracks only.

surfaces_walked.description: Which of the 10 attack surfaces the red-team explicitly considered. Names from the skill: input_boundaries, state_boundaries, sequence_boundaries, integration_boundaries, auth_edges, resource_exhaustion, failure_modes, abuse_cases, downstream_effects, observability_gaps. Empty surfaces (where nothing applied or nothing found) should still appear here — the explicit list is auditable.

findings_count.description: Total findings written into pipeline/red-team-report.md (sum of must-fix + should-fix + noted-for-followup).

severity_breakdown.description: Counts of findings by severity across all scopes (must-fix + should-fix + noted).

id.description: Stable id of the finding within the report, e.g. 'RT-3'.

surface.description: Which surface this finding came from.

summary.description: One-line description; the full reproducer lives in red-team-report.md.

must_address_before_peer_review.description: Items the implementer MUST address before Stage 5 peer review. Promotion rule (from the red-team skill): severity ≥ high AND likelihood ≥ plausible AND scope = must-fix. Non-empty → gate status FAIL. Each item also appears in the base gate's `blockers` array.

noted_for_followup.description: Findings out-of-scope for this PR but worth tracking. Surfaced to Stage 9 retrospective for pattern-spotting; can be promoted to follow-up tickets via the team's normal process.
